source: Claude session audit (2026-05-29) — deep-dive on T-143
title: Normie mode has no real conversation history — single_message_ctx + tiny session_ctx window
what_failed: Even after the T-148 extractor fix, normie still cannot hold a coherent multi-turn conversation. normie runs single_message_ctx=True, so only the CURRENT user turn is sent to Groq as the messages array. Its entire memory of the dialogue is the session_ctx text blob built from extract_text_from_messages(n=10) — the last 10 messages (~5 turns), each clipped to 300 chars, injected into the system prompt. Anything older than 5 turns, or longer than 300 chars, is invisible to normie.
where_failed: pi_agent.py:979-983 (llm_messages = single message when single_message_ctx), pi_agent.py:956-961 (session_ctx_inject), agent/truncation.py extract_text_from_messages n=10 / [:300] clip
why_likely: single_message_ctx was introduced to protect the Groq free-tier 100k TPD budget (T-074 era). The cost optimization was correct; the side effect — normie has a 5-turn, 300-char goldfish memory — was never measured against coherence. T-143's own proposed fix ('bump keep_recent to 8') targets the wrong knob: normie never uses keep_recent.
migration_plan: 1. Decide the real working-memory budget for normie. Groq llama-3.3-70b has a 128k context window; the 100k/day TPD limit is the actual constraint, not per-turn context.

2. Option A (recommended): pass a real (truncated) message array to Groq instead of single_message_ctx. Reuse self.messages truncated to ~12-16 messages. This gives Groq native multi-turn context and removes the lossy 300-char session_ctx reconstruction entirely.

3. Option B (cheaper, smaller change): keep single_message_ctx but make the session_ctx window mode-configurable — raise n to ~16 and the per-message clip to ~800 chars for normie. Add fields to ModeConfig: session_ctx_turns, session_ctx_char_clip.
4. Add a TPD-budget estimate so whichever option ships still respects the daily Groq limit (router already tracks TPD per T-084 — reuse it).
5. Add a multi-turn coherence test in normie mode (depends on T-152 harness): state a fact at turn 1, reference it at turn 7, assert the fact is in the prompt Groq receives.
implementation_notes: This is the structural half of T-143. T-148 fixed the bug (assistant turns were being dropped entirely); this ticket fixes the design limit (even with the fix, the window is tiny and string-reconstructed). Do T-148 + verify coherence improves before deciding how aggressive to be here.
analysis: PERSPECTIVE: This is the single most important open ticket for making Pi feel like Pi. The whole project's thesis is 'an agent that remembers' — and the default, most-used mode forgets everything past 5 turns. I'd take Option A. The 300-char string-reconstruction in session_ctx is a workaround for a problem (token cost) that a real truncated message array plus the existing TPD tracker solves more cleanly and with higher fidelity. You're maintaining a lossy parallel context format for no benefit now that the router tracks budget. Kill it.
